signals.title: NZD/CAD — G8 Bilateral Theme + China Trade Shock
signals.text: NZD/CAD at 0.4348 (implied) — NZD leads G8 performance following China's 14.1% export surge, which bolsters proxy demand for the Kiwi. CAD remains the G8 laggard as Rabobank warns of war-related growth reshuffling despite WTI at $97.9. RBNZ 2.25% vs BoC 2.25% (0bp) carry neutrality shifts focus entirely to the growth divergence. Strong bilateral theme confirms NZD outperformance across the complex.

signals.title: AUD/USD — Positioning + Retail Divergence
signals.text: AUD/USD at 0.7235 — COT LF net long +58,994 (78.3% long) indicates heavy institutional backing for the RBA's hiking cycle. Retail sentiment at 88% short creates a massive contrarian divergence; retail is aggressively fading the institutional trend. RBA 4.35% vs Fed 3.75% (+60bp) carry premium supports the long bias. Retail capitulation risk is high if price tests the 0.7300 handle.

signals.title: NZD/USD — COT vs OIS Divergence + RBNZ
signals.text: NZD/USD at 0.5952 — COT LF net short -19,294 (specs short) contradicts the RBNZ hike bias ahead of the 27 May meeting. With NZD long% at an extreme low of 18.5%, the pair is highly sensitive to hawkish RBNZ rhetoric. A hawkish hold or explicit hike signal triggers a crowded spec short unwind. Watch for a break above 0.6000 to confirm the squeeze.

signals.title: EUR/USD — Correlation Break + Carry
signals.text: EUR/USD at 1.1766 — Correlation with EuroStoxx 50 at +0.708 (z=+1.86σ) marks a significant break from the -0.135 norm. EUR is trading as a pro-cyclical risk asset rather than a funding currency despite the -175bp carry deficit vs USD. Fed 3.75% vs ECB 2.0% remains the structural ceiling. Watch US CPI on 12 May to see if the correlation realigns with yield differentials.
